Post_Text: We’re opening our second engineering center in São Paulo, Brazil. 🇧🇷 This strategic hub will be home to hundreds of engineers specializing in privacy and safety, and includes the first Google Safety Engineering Center and Accessibility Discovery Center in Latin America. The space also is a tribute to the legacy of the late Luiz André Barroso, a Google leader whose pioneering work paved the way for Brazilian engineering excellence. The building is an adaptive reuse of a historic 1940s building, and we drew inspiration from Brazil’s rich heritage in our design details — like acoustic panels featuring hand-woven textiles from Minas Gerais weavers, whose traditional craft mirrors the binary logic of modern technology. Take a look inside → https://goo.gle/43yYBXg

We’re introducing Gemini 3.5 — our latest family of AI models combining frontier intelligence with action. We’re starting the rollout with Gemini 3.5 Flash, our strongest agentic and coding model yet. Whether you're writing complex code or streamlining your daily workflow, we designed this model to help you get more done. It can tackle heavy-lifting tasks at four times the speed of comparable AI models — often at less than half the cost. Because Gemini 3.5 Flash is now our default model, you'll experience this leap in speed and intelligence across the tools you already use, from the Gemini app to AI Mode in Search and across our developer platforms. Learn more → goo.gle/4nFp9iY

Earlier this year we announced AI Inbox in Gmail to help you better manage and stay on top of your inbox by intelligently prioritizing your most important updates and time-sensitive tasks securely. At Google I/O we shared our latest updates to AI Inbox, including: 📧 AI Inbox will generate personalized draft replies so you can review and respond in seconds. 📋 Managing your to-do list in a helpful homebase screen. Mark individual tasks done, dismiss unhelpful suggestions, and mark emails read in a single click. 📂 When you need to review a Google Doc, Sheet or Slide, the relevant link is surfaced right next to your to-do item.

Today, we announced a new go-to hub for your health and wellness, plus a brand new wearable. 💪 The Google Health app combines everything you know and love about the Fitbit experience with advanced new capabilities and better insights, so you can understand the "why" behind your health trends. It’s free to use and integrates with hundreds of other apps and devices, like your Peloton or meal-logging apps. 📈 After six months in Public Preview, the Google Health Coach will also start rolling out to Google Health Premium subscribers in the coming weeks, offering even more personalized analysis and suggestions. Google Health Premium benefits will also be included in Google AI Pro and Ultra plans. 👟 We’re also introducing our first screenless wearable: the Fitbit Air. It’s simple, comfortable and notification-free, with a battery life of up to a week and a variety of band styles for every mood and occasion. Available for pre-order today starting at $99.99. Learn more about all the updates coming to Google Health → https://goo.gle/49icyMA * Works with most phones running on Android 11 or higher and Apple iOS 16.4 or higher. Requires Google Account and Google Health app. Not intended for medical purposes. See g.co/health/fitbit-air for details. * Battery life depends upon many factors and usage and actual battery life may be lower. See g.co/fitbit/battery.

Think you need to be an expert before you graduate? Software Engineer Abena says otherwise. “Being a student is the time to try as many things as possible and to ask questions,” she says. “Your experiences are what will set you apart. Both your successes and failures will bring unique perspectives to what you do in the future.” Her key takeaway? Focus on asking questions and building a broad range of experiences. Abena, now working on Colossus, our distributed file system, focuses on reliability and performance at scale. Her favorite part of the job is how her team collaborates to solve massive, real-world challenges. “No problem is too big for us, and I learn something new every day,” she says. “It’s rewarding to know that what I do can improve the lives of people around the world.” Interested in a career like Abena’s? Explore our open roles → https://goo.gle/4noFyra

Yann LeCun is right: we can't fit a trillion synapses on a single piece of silicon. But the true limitation isn't just the silicon—it's the latency between the chips. By significantly reducing the communication barriers between logical nodes, we allow a thousand separate processors to function as a single, continuous neural surface. We aren't just connecting computers; we are minimizing the physical and logical boundaries that currently force us into inefficient hardware multiplexing. hashtag #AI, hashtag #ArtificialIntelligence, hashtag #DeepLearning, hashtag #Compute, hashtag #interconnects, hashtag #DataCenter, hashtag #CPO

Voxel Energy builds data centers coupled with onsite power generation, enabling data center commissioning in months, not years. $3 Trillion in data center projects are slated for construction by 2030, yet half may never be built due to power constraints. Voxel bypasses the gridlock throttling the American AI boom and unlocks billions in pull-in revenue. Voxel leverages the ever-expanding pool of second-life EV batteries to build energy storage capacity into the hundreds of megawatt-hours and beyond. These batteries, coupled with solar and Voxel's novel DC microgrid architecture, enable grid-scale power without the grid-scale delays. The founding team is composed of ex-Tesla engineers and data center construction managers with recognition for commercial EV manufacturing in the US. Voxel’s systems are live, operational, and already running off-grid compute. Batteries are being amassed to accommodate demand. And hundreds of acres are already under contract. Reservations are now open for existing sites. Capacity is limited. 🛰️ Legacy optical satellites are blind nearly 70% of the time due to clouds and darkness. Meanwhile, radar satellites can see through weather, but the data is difficult to interpret and doesn’t work with standard vision systems. AxionOrbital Space (YC W26) is solving this by translating raw radar signals into analysis-ready optical imagery in real time, enabling persistent 24/7 visibility for applications like defense, commodities, and disaster response, even when the sky is blocked! Imagine the capabilities. Launch video: https://lnkd.in/gBXgva5P Pioneer Fund is pumped to support Atharva Peshkar and Dhenenjay Yadav on their journey!

Update: Dhenenjay and I got into Y Combinator W26 (after getting rejected and then reversing it) to build AxionOrbital Space (YC W26). At AxionOrbital Space (YC W26), we're building foundation models to generate high-resolution optical satellite imagery from Radar (SAR). For decades, the space industry has had a dirty secret: 70% of the time, satellites are blind. Clouds, smoke, and darkness turn billion-dollar sensors into expensive, useless metal. The result? Earth observation is a lottery. You pay to task a satellite. You wait days. If it rains, you paid for a black square. We fixed the game. We use deterministic generative AI to translate Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR), which sees through everything, into crystal-clear optical images. Input: unintelligible radar noise. Output: photorealistic vision in 0.06 seconds. We are replacing "weather permitting" intelligence with ground truth. This means tracking military convoys through storms, counting oil tanks in the monsoon, and mapping flood lines at night, at 1/100th the cost of optical tasking. We are actively onboarding partners with critical monitoring needs (Defense, Finance, Disaster Response) and SAR data providers. It took a village to turn that "No" into a "Yes." Huge thanks to our YC partners Tyler Bosmeny, Christina Gilbert, Gustaf Alströmer 🇺🇦, and James Evans for believing in the vision. A massive shoutout to everyone who supported us along the way: Akash Bansal, Eli Blee-Goldman, Haokun Qin, Joe Connolly, Neha Suresh, Ryan McLinko, Tobias Herber, Yotam Rosenbaum, Rachel ten Brink, Rohit Sirosh, Harsha Srijay, Avinash Joshi, Ajith Govind Satheesh, Leeds Rising, John Zeratsky, Sidharth Choraria, Philip Johnston, and James Fong. And special thanks to Danna Gurari, Rajshree Shrestha, Robin Cole, and Ranjan Kumar Ghosh for all the mentorship and guidance over the years. Watch us turn noise into signal below. 👇 https://lnkd.in/gpphfMcY hashtag #YCombinator hashtag #DeepTech hashtag #Space hashtag #AI hashtag #AxionOrbital hashtag #Startups

Weather models predict the atmosphere, but they don't tell you what actually happens on the ground. AxionOrbital Space (YC W26) is building NeoEarth, a world model for Earth Observation that forecasts how the ground will change up to a month in advance. Instead of just taking static snapshots, NeoEarth maintains a persistent state for locations on Earth, updating continuously as new satellite data arrives. By conditioning these dynamics on climate variables, it can accurately forecast critical events like flood risks, deforestation, and crop growth. To prove it works, the founders of AxionOrbital backtested their model on Hurricane Helene. Using NeoEarth, they predicted the hurricane's appearance and trajectory more accurately than the national forecasters did at the time. They're shifting Earth Observation from asking "What is in this image?" to answering "What will happen here next?" Congrats on the launch, Dhenenjay Yadav and Atharva Peshkar!

Optical satellites fail about 70% of the time, yet defense strategies and market decisions depend on visibility that can be blocked by clouds and darkness. Modern intelligence demands reliable ground truth in any weather. AxionOrbital Space (YC W26) has built advanced SAR-to-Optical foundation models on Earth to solve this problem. Their Deterministic One-Step architecture resets the SOTA with an FID of 30.24 and SSIM of 0.6 by anchoring generation to physical spatial priors that strictly constrain outputs to reality. Their models, Hubble (10m resolution & Open Source) and Orion (0.5m & Restricted), achieve 0.06s inference latency for live monitoring. From air-gapped servers to the satellite edge, you can now track any asset through day, night, or storm. Congrats on the launch, Dhenenjay Yadav & Atharva Peshkar! 🚀 https://lnkd.in/g6ypdKnM

Introducing the world's first MCP Server that enables any LLM (via an MCP Client) to analyze petabyte-scale geospatial/satellite data and build end-to-end GIS models (including training, testing, and validation) in minutes. Today we are launching 'Axion Planetary MCP'. First of its kind, that connects LLMs with live satellite, ground sensors & a lot of different data feeds. It uses Google Cloud Platform for compute, processing and storage. I've been watching AI Agents emerge in every vertical, and I thought, "Why not Geospatial Agents?" At AxionOrbital Space (YC W26), we're building the world's first Agentic IDE to create highly complex geospatial models without spending millions on compute or consulting. This MCP server is a critical first step and offers a preview of what that agent-driven experience will feel like. This project is the foundation for autonomous Geo-Agents, and it's completely Open Source. You can either set up a local server (Stdio) or connect with our SSE endpoint, which will be free for everyone to access for the next month. We’d love feedback from the geospatial and AI community — give the repo a ⭐, try it out, and contribute if you’re excited about pushing geospatial AI forward. Repo : https://lnkd.in/dCxsDhXG P.S. Shoutout to Robin Cole for guiding me on Open Source software. hashtag #Geospatial hashtag #AI hashtag #LLM hashtag #OpenSource hashtag #EarthObservation hashtag #GIS hashtag #Developer hashtag #GoogleCloud hashtag #BigData hashtag #AgenticIDE hashtag #AxionOrbital
